constraints: An array BBB of length MMM is said to be dominating if each of its prefixes and suffixes has a majority element.
Formally,

For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \leq i \leq M1‚Č§i‚Č§M), in the prefix [B1,B2,‚Ä¶,Bi][B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_i][B1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Bi‚Äč], there should exist an element that appears strictly more than ‚Ćäi2‚Ćč\left\lfloor\frac{i}{2}\right\rfloor‚Ćä2i‚Äč‚Ćč times.

For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \leq i \leq M1‚Č§i‚Č§M), in the suffix [Bi,Bi+1,‚Ä¶,BM][B_i, B_{i+1}, \ldots, B_M][Bi‚Äč,Bi+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äč], there should exist an element that appears strictly more than ‚ĆäM‚ąíi+12‚Ćč\left\lfloor \frac{M-i+1}{2} \right\rfloor‚Ćä2M‚ąíi+1‚Äč‚Ćč times.
Here, ‚Ćäx‚Ćč\left\lfloor x \right\rfloor‚Ćäx‚Ćč denotes the largest integer that doesn't exceed xxx.
For example, ‚Ćä52‚Ćč=2\left\lfloor \frac{5}{2} \right\rfloor = 2‚Ćä25‚Äč‚Ćč=2 and ‚Ćä62‚Ćč=3\left\lfloor \frac{6}{2} \right\rfloor = 3‚Ćä26‚Äč‚Ćč=3.
Note that it is allowed for different prefixes and suffixes of BBB to have different majorities - the only condition is that they must each have a majority.
You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Count the number of its prefixes that are dominating.
That is, count the number of integers iii such that 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N, and the subarray
[A1,A2,‚Ä¶,Ai][A_1, A_{2}, \ldots, A_i][A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai‚Äč] is dominating.
Input Format
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the number of elements in the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of the array.
Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of dominating prefixes.
1‚Č§N‚Č§2‚čÖ1051 \leq N \leq 2\cdot 10^51‚Č§N‚Č§2‚čÖ105
1‚Č§Ai‚Č§N1 \leq A_i \leq N1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§N
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
